member.name: T:ContractCallQuery
member.summary:
Call a function of the given smart contract instance, giving it functionParameters as its inputs. It will consume the entire given amount of gas.

This is performed locally on the particular node that the client is communicating with. It cannot change the state of the contract instance (and so, cannot spend anything from the instance's cryptocurrency account).

It will not have a consensus timestamp. It cannot generate a record or a receipt. The response will contain the output returned by the function call. This is useful for calling getter functions, which purely read the state and don't change it. It is faster and cheaper than a normal call, because it is purely local to a single node.

member.name: P:ContractCallQuery.Gas
member.summary:
Sets the amount of gas to use for the call.

All of the gas offered will be charged for.

The long to be set as gas {@code this}

member.name: M:ContractCallQuery.CopyArray
member.summary:
Sets the function parameters as their raw bytes.

Use this instead of {@link #setFunction(String, ContractFunctionParameters)} if you have already pre-encoded a solidity function call.

The function parameters to be set {@code this}

member.name: P:ContractCallQuery.MaXResultSize
member.summary:
The long to be set as size {@code this} @deprecatedwith no replacement
Sets the max number of bytes that the result might include. The run will fail if it had returned more than this number of bytes.

member.name: P:ContractCallQuery.SenderAccountId
member.summary: Sets the account that is the "sender." If not present it is the accountId from the transactionId. Typically a different value than specified in the transactionId requires a valid signature over either the hedera transaction or foreign transaction data.

member.name: M:ContractCallQuery.SetFunction(System.String)
member.summary:
Sets the function name to call.

The function will be called with no parameters. Use {@link #setFunction(String, ContractFunctionParameters)} to call a function with parameters.

The function name to be set {@code this}

member.name: M:ContractCallQuery.SetFunction(System.String,ContractFunctionParameters @)
member.summary: Sets the function to call, and the parameters to pass to the function.
member.param: The function name to be set
